meta.manuscript_location: Shrine of the Book, Israel Museum, Jerusalem

preamble.summary: Isaiah 4 is a short chapter (6 verses) that moves from the aftermath of judgment to the promise of restoration — the Branch of the LORD and the protective canopy over Zion. 1QIsaiah-a agrees very closely with the MT throughout this chapter.
preamble.notable_variants: No significant variants. The 'Branch of the LORD' (tsemach YHWH) in verse 2 reads identically in both traditions.
preamble.column_reference: Column IV of 1QIsaiah-a contains Isaiah chapter 4.

variant_notes: No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.

Identical consonantal text. The messianic 'Branch of the LORD' (tsemach YHWH) reads the same in 1QIsaiah-a. This term becomes a key messianic title in later prophets (Jeremiah 23:5, 33:15; Zechariah 3:8, 6:12).
No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.
Identical consonantal text. The imagery of the protective cloud over Zion matches the MT exactly.
No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.
